geography.data_source: Census 2011 ward boundary shapefiles, Delhi district Shahdara

demographics.data_source: Census 2011 ward-level tables (D-Series), Shahdara district. Density = 1710000 / 57.8 ≈ 29585

income_profile.data_source: NSSO 78th Round MPCE distribution (Delhi East district), Census 2011 B-Series worker tables. Left-skewed: Shahdara is predominantly lower-middle income with significant informal manufacturing and trade employment.

commute_profile.data_source: DMRC Origin-Destination Survey 2022, Census 2011 Table D-13 (mode of travel to work). Low car ownership (0.18) correlates with high metro dependency (0.74) and high fare sensitivity.

sensitivity_parameters.data_source: Composite scores derived from: NSSO 78th Round expenditure data (fare sensitivity), TRAI Digital India Report + IAMAI Internet Report (diffusion), Lokniti-CSDS 2024 disaggregated by income class (institutional trust), ECI 2024 Lok Sabha turnout + margin data for Shahdara constituency (political mobilisation). World Values Survey India Wave 7 (loss aversion).

social_network_parameters.data_source: Estimated from Census 2011 household density and IHDS-II survey social capital module. High clustering (0.68) reflects tight residential colony structure in Trans-Yamuna. Low cross-income bridging (0.12) reflects income homogeneity.

informal_economy_nodes.data_source: Estimated from MCD market license data and DMRC station exit surveys. Shahdara main market near Shahdara metro station.
Estimated from MCD commercial zone records. Gandhi Nagar wholesale garment market cluster, one of Asia's largest garment wholesale markets.
Estimated from local survey data. Geeta Colony market area serving residential population.

tier1_agent_archetype_weights.data_source: CITY_PROFILES.md lines 333-340 — zone-specific archetype distribution for Shahdara. Sum = 1.00. These weights reflect Shahdara's character as a transit-dependent lower-middle-income zone with high informal self-employment and significant student population.

tier2_agent_types.data_source: Estimated from MCD ward councillor count (8 wards = 1 councillor representative), RWA registration data from Delhi Government portal, and Indian Newspaper Society local press statistics.
